kept.text:
Israel gave final approval on Sunday for a plan to purchase two new combat squadrons of F-35 and F-15IA aircraft from Lockheed Martin and Boeing, its defense ministry said.

The deal, worth tens of billions of shekels, includes acquiring advanced fighter jets to boost Israel’s air capabilities.

profile_bio.description: The world's leading news network on Iran. Follow us for fearless journalism, firsthand reports and accurate, in-depth coverage

kept.text:
Ministry of Defense and IDF to acquire two new combat squadrons of F-35 and F-15IA aircraft as part of Israel’s 350 billion shekel force build-up plan.

The procurement of a fourth squadron of the F-35 Adir, manufactured by Lockheed Martin, and a second squadron of F-15IA aircraft, manufactured by Boeing is part of ‘Magen Israel’, Israel’s central force build-up plan to ensure the Israeli military’s qualitative edge, Defmin Katz said. 

“As part of the plan, the Air Force will also be required to lead a technological leap forward, in the development and integration of autonomous flight capabilities, next-generation advanced defense systems, and the establishment of Israeli military superiority, defensive and offensive, in space,” said Katz.

MoD Dir. Gen. (Ret.) Majgen Amir Baram said “The approval of the combat squadron procurement is the first and central step in implementing the 350 billion shekel force build-up plan for a complex security decade.”

kept.text:
🇨🇦 Canada just quietly hopped on the HIMARS train.

With zero public announcement, Ottawa got added to a massive $1.1 billion U.S. contract with Lockheed Martin for M142 HIMARS rocket systems.

17 launchers are being built by 2028, and Canada is one of the buyers (they originally wanted up to 26).

After years of dragging its feet on military modernization, Canada is finally getting one of the most lethal and battle-proven long-range artillery systems out there.

Quietly going big on HIMARS. Interesting move. 

Source: CBC News
Media: Archive footage

kept.text:
⭕🇨🇦🇺🇸 Canada Quietly Joins U.S. HIMARS Deal

Canada has been silently included in a $1.1B U.S. contract with Lockheed Martin for M142 HIMARS, with no official announcement.

17 systems will be built by 2028, with Canada among the buyers.

Ottawa earlier aimed for up to 26 launchers.

Battlefield proven system now heading to Canadian forces

Source: CBC News

kept.text:
(Reuters) - Israel gave final approval for a plan to purchase two new combat squadrons of F-35 and F-15IA advanced fighter aircraft from Lockheed Martin and Boeing in a deal worth tens of billions of dollars, the defence ministry said on Sunday.

The deal, approved by Israel's Ministerial Committee on Procurement, is a first step in a 350 billion shekel ($119 billion) plan to bolster Israel's military and "strengthen readiness ahead of a demanding decade for Israeli security," it said.

kept.text:
Israel’s Defense Ministry announced that the Ministerial Committee on Procurement has given final approval for a major $119 billion deal to acquire two new fighter jet squadrons from the United States. The purchase includes a fourth squadron of F-35I Adir from Lockheed Martin, as well as a second squadron of F-15IA from Boeing.

With an estimated 25 aircraft per squadron in the Israeli Air Force, this acquisition would add roughly 50 advanced jets to Israel’s fleet.
